pattern: The shortest path between any two nodes in a tree always goes up to their lowest common ancestor and back down, so recording the root-to-node path for each target and trimming their shared prefix gives the answer directly, no explicit LCA search needed.
hints: There are no shortcuts between siblings or cousins in a tree -- any path from one node to another must climb up to their common ancestor first, then descend.
Find the root-to-node path (as a sequence of L/R moves) for the start value and, separately, for the destination value.

Strip off the longest common prefix shared by both paths -- that prefix is the climb to the lowest common ancestor, so it doesn't appear in the answer. What's left of the start's path becomes 'U' moves (climbing up), and what's left of the destination's path is used as-is (descending down).

edge_inputs.description: statement example, path climbs up twice then descends right then left
statement example, destination is the immediate parent
destination is the root itself, path is all upward moves
start is the root, path is all downward moves
start and destination are siblings, one up then one down
start is an ancestor of destination several levels down
destination is an ancestor of start several levels up
deep left-skewed chain, longest possible pure ascent to the root

stress.description: random complete-ish binary trees with a random distinct start and destination value
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    from collections import deque
    n = max(2, min(size, 5000))
    values = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(values)
    root_val = values[0]
    nodes = {root_val: [None, None]}
    open_slots = [(root_val, 0), (root_val, 1)]
    for v in values[1:]:
        idx = rng.randrange(len(open_slots))
        parent, side = open_slots.pop(idx)
        nodes[parent][side] = v
        nodes[v] = [None, None]
        open_slots.append((v, 0))
        open_slots.append((v, 1))
    out = []
    q = deque([root_val])
    while q:
        cur = q.popleft()
        if cur is None:
            out.append(None)
            continue
        out.append(cur)
        l, r = nodes[cur]
        q.append(l)
        q.append(r)
    while out and out[-1] is None:
        out.pop()
    start, dest = rng.sample(values, 2)
    return [out, start, dest]
stress.note: random binary tree built by attaching each value to a random open child slot, serialized BFS level-order with trailing nulls trimmed; start/dest are distinct existing values
